seoTitle: How to Sign Into Slack on Phone From Desktop (QR Code)
metaDescription: Skip typing passwords by signing into Slack mobile using the desktop app's QR code scanner for instant cross-device authentication.
intro: Typing complex passwords and workspace URLs on a small mobile screen is tedious. This guide shows how to use Slack's built-in QR code feature to sign into the mobile app instantly using your already authenticated desktop session.

steps.heading: Open your workspace menu
steps.body: Click your workspace name or logo at the top of the left sidebar in the Slack desktop app.

steps.heading: Select Sign In on Mobile
steps.body: Hover over your organization profile and click 'Sign in on Mobile' to open the QR code panel.

steps.heading: Display the QR code
steps.body: Keep the unique, single-use QR code visible on your desktop screen.

steps.heading: Open the Slack mobile app
steps.body: Launch the Slack app on your phone and tap the 'Scan QR Code' option on the sign-in screen.

steps.heading: Scan and authenticate
steps.body: Grant camera access, align your phone with the code on your monitor, and hold steady until the sign-in completes.

faq.question: Does the QR code expire?
faq.answer: Yes, each generated QR code is single-use and expires after a short time for security.

faq.question: Can I use this to sign into multiple workspaces on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, you can repeat the process for each additional workspace you need on mobile.

faq.question: What if my phone doesn't have a QR scanner in the app?
faq.answer: Make sure you're using an updated version of the Slack mobile app, as older versions may lack this feature.

faq.question: Is scanning a QR code more secure than typing a password?
faq.answer: It avoids exposing your password on a mobile keyboard and ties authentication to a device you're already logged into.
